title: [codex] remove stale image tunnel bootstrap files
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the tunnels
gist.blurb: We did some spring cleaning in our infrastructure scripts, removing outdated Cloudflare tunnel setups to keep our codebase tidy and reliable.
gist.summary: Removed stale and duplicate Cloudflare tunnel bootstrap scripts from the image generation backend to prevent configuration drift.
gist.impact: Developers setting up local or new environments will now use a single, reliable setup script without conflicting configurations.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about removing old, stale tunnel files (Cloudflare tunnels). Visual metaphor: Polly the bee sweeping or cleaning up old dusty tunnels or pipes, while the Nomnom creature eats the old deprecated code files.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny bandana, sweeping dust out of a small lime green (#ecf874) pipe, while the round tan nomnom creature happily eats a stack of old dusty code documents. Soft pastel lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
